title: TV-Watching Habits
description: Write a program that asks the user how long on average they spend watching TV each day. If it is less than 2 hours, output 'That shouldn't rot your brain too much'; if it is less than 4 hours per day, output 'Aren't you getting square eyes?'; otherwise output 'Fresh air beats channel flicking'.
example:
Input: Enter average daily TV hours: 3
Output:
Aren't you getting square eyes?

title: Random Dice Roll
description: Generate a random integer between 1 and 6 and print the result to simulate a dice roll.
solution:
import random
roll = random.randint(1, 6)
print(f"Rolled a {roll}")

title: Guess the Number
description: Ask the user to guess a secret number between 1 and 10. Keep asking until they guess it correctly.
example:
Input: Enter your guess: 3
(Repeated prompts until correct)
Output:
You guessed it!
solution:
secret = 7
guess = 0
while guess != secret:
    guess = int(input("Enter your guess (1-10): "))
print("You guessed it!")

title: Print Prime Numbers
description: Ask the user for a number n. Print all prime numbers from 2 up to n.

title: Password Prompt
description: Ask the user for a password and keep asking until the correct password is entered. Then display 'Access granted'.
example:
Input: Enter password: 123
Wrong! Enter password: openSesame
Output:
Access granted
solution:
correct = "openSesame"
pw = ""
while pw != correct:
    pw = input("Enter password: ")
    if pw != correct:
        print("Wrong!")
print("Access granted")

title: Even or Odd (Loop)
description: Continuously ask the user for numbers. For each number, print whether it is even or odd. Stop when the user enters 0.
example:
Input: 4 -> Output: even
Input: 7 -> Output: odd
Input: 0 -> stops

title: Dice Total
description: Simulate rolling two dice (1-6) and print their individual values plus the total.
solution:
import random
d1 = random.randint(1, 6)
d2 = random.randint(1, 6)
print(f"Dice1 = {d1}, Dice2 = {d2}, Total = {d1 + d2}")

title: Store 5 Numbers
description: Ask the user for 5 numbers, store them in a list, and print the list.
solution:
nums = []
for i in range(5):
    val = int(input("Enter a number: "))
    nums.append(val)
print(nums)

title: Coin Flip
description: Simulate flipping a coin. Print 'Heads' or 'Tails' at random.
solution:
import random
flip = random.choice(["Heads", "Tails"])
print(flip)

title: Remove Spaces
description: Ask the user for a sentence and remove all spaces from it.
solution:
text = input("Enter a sentence: ")
print(text.replace(" ", ""))

title: Sum Until Zero
description: Repeatedly ask the user for a number until they enter 0. Then print the sum of all entered numbers (excluding the 0).
solution:
total = 0
while True:
    num = int(input("Enter a number (0 to stop): "))
    if num == 0:
        break
    total += num
print("Sum =", total)

title: Multiplication Table (1-10)
description: Print a multiplication table from 1x1 up to 10x10.

title: Sort Names
description: Ask the user for a list of names (comma-separated) and print them in alphabetical order.
solution:
names_str = input("Enter names (comma-separated): ")
names = [n.strip() for n in names_str.split(",")]
names.sort()
print(names)

title: Filter Even from List
description: Ask the user for a list of numbers (comma-separated). Print only the even ones.

title: Palindrome Checker
description: Ask the user for a word and check if it is a palindrome (the same forwards and backwards).

title: Pass/Fail Average
description: Ask the user for their marks in 3 subjects, calculate the average, and tell them if they passed (average >= 50).

title: Squares of 1-10
description: Use a loop to print the squares of numbers from 1 to 10.
solution:
for i in range(1, 11):
    print(i*i, end=" ")

title: Star Pyramid
description: Ask the user for a number n, then print a pyramid of stars of height n.
solution:
n = int(input("Enter height: "))
for i in range(n):
    stars = 2*i + 1
    spaces = n - i - 1
    print(" "*spaces + "*"*stars)

title: Words on New Lines
description: Ask the user for a sentence and print each word on a new line.
example:
Input: Hello world program
Output:
Hello
world
program
solution:
sentence = input("Enter a sentence: ")
words = sentence.split()
for w in words:
    print(w)

title: Random Password Generator
description: Generate an 8-character random password using letters, digits, and special characters.
solution:
import random
import string
chars = string.ascii_letters + string.digits + "!@#$%^&*"
password = "".join(random.choice(chars) for _ in range(8))
print(password)

title: Count a Letter
description: Ask the user for a string and a letter, then count how many times that letter appears.
solution:
text = input("Enter a string: ")
letter = input("Enter a letter: ")
count = text.count(letter)
print(count)

title: Print Even Numbers
description: Ask the user for a number and print all even numbers from 1 to that number.

title: Print Odd Numbers
description: Ask the user for a number and print all odd numbers from 1 to that number.

title: Simple Calculator
description: Write a program that can add, subtract, multiply, or divide two numbers based on user choice.

title: Password Strength Checker
description: Ask the user for a password and check if it has at least 8 characters, includes digits, and has both upper and lower case letters.
example:
Input: Abc12345
Output:
Strong password!
(If missing criteria, show which ones are missing.)
